global: Durability meta dialed back: tower, inhibitor, minion, and jungle monster health/damage increased across the board to make pushing and objective trades slower and more deliberate (e.g. Outer Tower 5000->5800 HP, Inner 4000->5000, Inhibitor 3800->4500; Super Minion HP 1600-4820->1600-5240 but its damage to inhibitors/core reduced).

Patch-wide kit theme: many physical-scaling heroes gained Magical Power / Bonus Physical Power scaling on abilities, opening hybrid and AP-bruiser build paths (Akeron, Boris, Greystone, Grux, Howitzer, Khaimera, Kwang, Rampage, Riktor, Shinbi, Yin, Zarus, and others).
items: Crests: Razorback Echidna armor bonus 25%->20%, cooldown 90->100s.
Carry: Sky Splitter lifesteal 7%->8% (melee dmg 6->5.5%, ranged 4->3.5%); Stormbreaker attack speed 35->30; Viper shred 4%->4.5% (+armor-correction bugfix).
Assassin: Deathstalker penetration 9->10; Infernum Cinder max-health 3%->4%; Nightfall omnivamp 7%->8%.

Fighter: Augmentation ability haste 16->20 / scaling 80->90%; Mutilator power 40->45 & omnivamp 10->12%; Vyzar-Carapace power 40->45 & health 200->225; Basilisk shred 4->4.5%; Solstice Mornfall 0-60%->0-55%.
Mage: Orb of Growth mana 300->250; Orb of Enlightenment mana 300->250 & scaling 4->3/level.
Tank: Cursed Scroll health scaling 5->4% & regen 30->25% (+Cleave-trigger bugfix).
Tier 2 / fixes: Ruthless Broadsword shred 4->4.5%; Refillable Potion (135-270) and Divine Potion (165-300) healing labels corrected.

eternals.summary: This patch's headline is a big rebalance of the Eternals (the deity blessings picked in draft). The durability and sustain blessings that defined the meta got trimmed, while a few scaling and economy paths were buffed. Here's what changed and how it affects your picks.

changes.name: Vermis
changes.change: Serration reworked: instead of amplifying critical strikes, it now adds 4% of the target's missing health as bonus damage on every proc. Cauterize and Sub-Surface were lightly trimmed.
changes.meaning: Still the go-to on-hit anti-tank Eternal, but Serration is now a steady, reliable damage add rather than a crit gamble — better on non-crit carries.

changes.name: Marrow
changes.change: The Maw and Leech swapped slots, so you can no longer run Blood Bank + Leech together. The Maw now triggers on all basic attacks (not just crits) below 30% HP, and Blood Bank's lifesteal per stack was trimmed.
changes.meaning: Default shifts to Blood Bank + The Maw — stack lifesteal and execute low targets. Take Leech instead when you need to shut off heavy enemy regen.

changes.name: Thraex
changes.change: Golden Mane's damage reduction dropped 12% → 8% but now applies to all non-AoE attacks (not just abilities); Rallying Roar now also grants Ultimate Haste.
changes.meaning: A bit less personal tankiness in duels, traded for more frequent ults.

changes.name: Nihil
changes.change: Growing Threat's bonus damage 15% → 12% but now also adds 5% omnivamp; From the Abyss's regen now scales with level.
changes.meaning: Slightly less burst, more lifesteal and scaling sustain for long extended fights.

changes.name: Krix
changes.change: The Major's bonus Max Health was halved (10% → 5%); Voracity, Regenerative Tissue and Cannibalism were all retuned.
changes.meaning: The signature 'giant HP tank' identity got reined in — still a frontline anchor, just not as oversized.

changes.name: Idrisil
changes.change: Mending's missing-health heal now scales by level (weaker early, stronger late).
changes.meaning: The armor-tank pick is a little softer in the early game and rewards getting to the late game.

changes.name: Xyris
changes.change: The Major is now front-loaded (flat 4% + 0.6% per 10 units instead of pure stacking). Bone Collector was reworked into a crit payoff (20% Crit Chance + 5% Crit Damage at 150 units); Iron Will now heals on all basics; Eviscerate 20% → 15%.
changes.meaning: Xyris is now a crit hyper-carry — Furor + Bone Collector turns farm into crit power. Build crit and farm to 150 units for the spike.

changes.name: Lotus
changes.change: The Major's damage/health rolls were trimmed; Immaculate's damage reduction 35% → 20%; Early Bloom now triggers at level 12 (was 10); Fragrance now adds a magic-damage amp.
changes.meaning: Still the safe, flexible pick, but its defensive payoffs are weaker — less of a free 'tanky' bonus when ahead.

changes.name: Demiurge
changes.change: Bloatware and Construct swapped slots; Bloatware's damage-per-item 1% → 0.5%; Construct reworked to per-level Health and Ability Haste.
changes.meaning: Still the late-game full-build scaler; the Construct + Bloatware combo is intact, just in opposite slots now.

changes.name: Aion
changes.change: Bottomless Vial, Perfection (now triggers above 85% HP), and Runic Focus were all buffed.
changes.meaning: Mana-economy mages convert mana into power more efficiently — stronger poke and scaling.

changes.name: Phylactery (Vesh)
changes.change: Soft rework: now grants 100% Cooldown Progression for 3s after your ult; shield 20% → 15% of Max Mana and duration 4s → 3s.
changes.meaning: Shifts from a pure shield into a cooldown-reset tool — cast your ult, then immediately get your other abilities back.

analysis.summary: Wraith was nerfed again: lower max-health damage on Peekaboo and reduced Knock Knock scaling.
analysis.playing: Your burst and anti-tank damage are down — pick engages carefully and itemize for the lost damage.
analysis.facing: His pick burst is weaker — a bit more survivable if caught, but still respect the engage.

analysis.summary: Kira's Mercy ability was nerfed, notably lower damage.
analysis.playing: Your poke and finishing damage from Mercy is down; rely more on positioning and basics.
analysis.facing: Her burst is lower — you can play a bit more forward against her poke.

analysis.summary: Narbash was nerfed: less durability and weaker team healing/regen from March and his ult.
analysis.playing: You keep the team alive a bit less and are squishier — position carefully and rely on CC/utility over raw healing.
analysis.facing: His sustain aura is weaker — diving his backline is a little safer now.

changes: Ability mana costs rebalanced (Gift 60-100->70-90, Fly 60-100->80-100, Sparkle 75->60-80)

analysis.summary: Mostly a mana-economy tune: a bigger mana pool and regen, with several ability costs shuffled — smoother sustain overall.
analysis.playing: You run out of mana less often; spend a little freer in lane, though some abilities cost slightly more.
analysis.facing: Little practical change — he can poke a bit more sustainably, but his damage is the same.

changes: Crushing Maw magical scaling 40%->45%, added 75% Bonus Physical Power
Consume bite and execute threshold now scale with Bonus Physical Power
Web Lash slam now scales 50% Bonus Physical Power

analysis.summary: Akeron's cooldowns dropped and his abilities now scale hard with bonus Physical Power on top of magical, so a damage build hits far harder than before.
analysis.playing: Build more aggressively for damage — your combo and Consume execute now reward stacking Physical Power, and lower Hellfire cooldown means more uptime.
analysis.facing: Respect a bigger burst window; don't sit in his web range at low health, since his execute threshold now scales with his power.

changes: Dire Howl added 65% Magical Power, omnivamp 6-10%->10-18%

analysis.summary: Reworked toward sustain and Magical Power: his attack-speed steroid was toned down, but his abilities now scale with Magical Power and his ult heals far more (omnivamp nearly doubled).
analysis.playing: This opens a hybrid/AP bruiser Bayle that sustains hard in long fights — lean into Dire Howl's healing and the new Magical scaling over pure attack speed.
analysis.facing: He sustains much more in drawn-out fights now — bring anti-heal and try to burst him rather than out-DPS his omnivamp.

changes: Added Magical Power scaling to Tesseract Device and Detection Protocol

analysis.summary: Boris's base stats and physical damage were cut, but Magical Power scaling was added across his kit — a nerf to standard builds that quietly opens an AP Boris.
analysis.playing: Pure-physical Boris is weaker; consider Magical Power, which now scales much of his kit, to recoup the lost damage.
analysis.facing: He's weaker and squishier on standard builds — pressure him, but note an AP Boris can still scale.

changes: Stone Forged Soul cooldown 165/150/135->175/160/145 (added 100% Magical Power, healing +2.5% Magical)
Sacred Oath mana 25->30, Make Way added 80% Magical

analysis.summary: Greystone was nerfed on durability and his ult cooldown went up, though Magical Power scaling was added — a hit to the standard tanky build.
analysis.playing: You're less tanky and your revive is up less often; AP scaling is now an option but the safe build is weaker.
analysis.facing: Punish him harder in lane and time your burst for when his revive ult is down.

changes: Bloodlust added 20% Magical to bleed, Warlord's Challenge steal added 1.5% Magical

analysis.summary: Grux's damage and self-healing were trimmed (with Magical scaling added to his bleed) — a modest nerf to his bruiser sustain.
analysis.playing: You heal and hit a little less; play the early game more carefully and consider some Magical Power for the bleed.
analysis.facing: His sustain is down slightly — anti-heal is still good and you can trade into him a bit more freely.

changes: Ancestral Fury added 0.3% Magical Power/stack, rage 3->4 per attack

analysis.summary: Big buff: Cull comes back dramatically faster and his kit now scales with Magical Power too, opening strong bruiser/AP jungle builds with far more ult uptime.
analysis.playing: You can fight and re-fight much more often thanks to the shorter Cull cooldown; experiment with Magical Power for extra sustain and damage.
analysis.facing: He'll have his execute ult up almost every skirmish now — avoid repeated low-health duels and bring anti-heal.

analysis.summary: Minor buff: Morigesh's Hive does more damage.
analysis.playing: Slightly stronger poke and mark detonations — play as usual.
analysis.facing: Her poke chips a bit harder; clear the Hive marks before she follows up.

changes: Behemoth movement speed added 10% Magical Power scaling

analysis.summary: Rampage now scales with Magical Power on his poke and ult movement — a buff that opens hybrid/AP builds.
analysis.playing: Try some Magical Power: Boulder Throw poke and Behemoth chase/escape both get stronger on top of his usual bruiser kit.
analysis.facing: His poke can hit harder than the old physical-only build suggested; don't assume low damage if he's gone AP.

changes: Added Bonus Physical scaling to Zap O'9-Chains (30%) and Shock Therapy (70%)

analysis.summary: Riktor was nerfed on durability and shield, with bonus Physical Power scaling added — a hit to tank Riktor that nudges toward a damage build.
analysis.playing: You're less tanky; the added Physical scaling rewards a more damage-oriented bruiser build to keep your threat up.
analysis.facing: Tank Riktor is squishier now — punish his hooks and burst him through the smaller shield.

changes: Reaper of Souls health/stack 3->1, tier bonuses 40/80/120/160->50/100/200/400

analysis.summary: Sevarog's stacking passive was reworked (less health per stack but bigger milestone bonuses) and his sustain and costs were nerfed — weaker early, more milestone-dependent.
analysis.playing: Early stacks give less health, so lane is rougher; push to the big stack-tier breakpoints and manage higher ability costs.
analysis.facing: He's weaker and hungrier for stacks early — deny his last-hits on minions and camps to slow his spikes.

changes: Heavy Metal total armor scaling decreased
Ruthless Assault armor scaling 40%->37.5% (empowered damage curve lowered)
Wild Rush minimum scaling 40%->37.5%, max charge 120%->112.5%

analysis.summary: Terra was nerfed: her armor-based scaling and empowered damage were trimmed.
analysis.playing: Your damage off armor is a bit lower — you're still tanky, but expect less burst from your steroid.
analysis.facing: She does a little less off her defensive stats — trading into her is slightly safer.

analysis.summary: Yurei was nerfed across the board: less health, lower ability damage and scaling, and a longer, more punishing ult.
analysis.playing: You're squishier and your ult is riskier (bigger self-slow, longer cooldown) — be deliberate with engages.
analysis.facing: His damage and ult uptime are down — you can play more aggressively and punish his slower ult.

changes: Eventide damage curve lowered at higher ranks

analysis.summary: Countess was nerfed: less durability and mana, lower max-health damage, weaker late Eventide, and a longer ult cooldown.
analysis.playing: You're more fragile and your ult is up less — pick your dives carefully and rely on less burst.
analysis.facing: She's easier to punish with more ult downtime — trade when Feast is down.

analysis.summary: Drongo's poke and ult hit harder and his ability is up more often — a clean carry buff.
analysis.playing: Lean into Old Rusty poke (the longer slow sets up follow-up) and trust your ult to do more late.
analysis.facing: His zoning is stronger; dodge the puddle or you'll eat a longer slow into more damage.

analysis.summary: Small, clean buff — Gadget's Sticky Mine simply does more damage at every rank.
analysis.playing: Your lane poke and combo damage are a touch higher; play the same, just trade a bit more confidently.
analysis.facing: Slightly riskier to walk over her mines — give them a wider berth in lane.

analysis.summary: Minor buff: Gideon's Void Breach deals more damage.
analysis.playing: A bit more poke and teamfight damage on your main zoning tool — nothing else changes.
analysis.facing: His poke stings a little more; don't get greedy standing in the void zone.

changes: Make It Rain added 120% Bonus Physical total

analysis.summary: Howitzer's abilities now scale much more with bonus Physical Power, so a full AD build turns him into a real damage threat.
analysis.playing: Build damage — your whole kit, especially the Make It Rain ult, scales far harder now; great for teamfight zoning.
analysis.facing: His combos and ult hit noticeably harder; don't group tightly in Make It Rain and pop him before he free-casts.

changes: Light of Heavens added 70% Bonus Physical (true dmg +80% Bonus Physical)

analysis.summary: Kwang's core abilities hit harder, reach further, and cool down faster, and his ult now scales with bonus Physical Power.
analysis.playing: Bigger, more frequent Judgement/Surge means stronger trades, and building Physical Power now boosts your ult.
analysis.facing: His engage range and trade damage went up — respect the larger Judgement hitbox and his ult burst.

analysis.summary: Belica gets more damage from her passive, basics, and Void Bomb — a clean numbers buff.
analysis.playing: Your mana-burn poke and combo hit harder; nothing changes mechanically, you just win trades more easily.
analysis.facing: Her poke and burst are stronger; don't let her freely Void Bomb you in lane.

changes: Crescendo added 25% Bonus Physical, Biting Melody added 10%
Circle Rhythm and Rushing Beat per-rank damage increased

analysis.summary: Across-the-board damage buff: her wolves, dash, and ult all scale better, and several abilities now also reward bonus Physical Power.
analysis.playing: Both AP and on-hit Shinbi got stronger — your wolves and ult clear and burst harder, so play aggressively.
analysis.facing: Her skirmish and dive damage are up; ward for the wolf roams and don't get caught alone.

analysis.summary: Small buff: TwinBlast's Vaporize and Vortex Grenade scale a bit better with Physical Power.
analysis.playing: A touch more ability damage in your combo — keep playing the mobile crit/AD carry.
analysis.facing: Marginally more burst from his abilities; otherwise the same matchup.

changes: Spear of Nyr damage 70-190->70-210, scaling 115%->125%, +25% monster damage reduction, added 80% Magical

analysis.summary: Big all-around buff: more Physical Power per level, a faster/longer Evade, a much stronger Spear of Nyr (now with monster damage reduction and Magical scaling), and a bigger ult.
analysis.playing: He's stronger in lane and jungle — your spear pokes harder, you survive camps better, the ult does more, and Magical Power is now an option.
analysis.facing: Expect harder spear poke and a bigger ult; don't trade into his Evade and respect his improved survivability.

changes: Flame Turret melee modifier +50%->+70% but scaling 10-36%->10-32.5%, mana 20->30

analysis.summary: Iggy got squishier and his lane poke was trimmed, but his Inferno ult got stronger — a shift from safe poke toward ult-focused teamfighting.
analysis.playing: You're more fragile, so play safer early and lean on your stronger Inferno in fights; watch Flame Turret mana.
analysis.facing: Punish him early while he's squishier, but respect a bigger Inferno in teamfights — don't clump in the flames.

analysis.summary: Mixed: her healing was slightly cut, but Ricochet is now easier to land and fires faster — more reliable damage, a bit less sustain.
analysis.playing: Your poke and combo are snappier and more consistent; just don't rely on quite as much healing.
analysis.facing: Her Ricochet is harder to dodge now — sidestep earlier; her heals are slightly weaker too.

changes: Glacial Thorns magical scaling 30%->20%, root 0.6s->0.5s (hitbox trimmed)

analysis.summary: Aurora was nerfed: less ability damage, a shorter root, and smaller hitboxes that make her skillshots harder to land.
analysis.playing: Combos are tighter to land and do less — focus on clean Glacial Thorns roots, but expect lower overall damage.
analysis.facing: Her skillshots are easier to dodge and her root is shorter — play around her abilities more aggressively.

analysis.summary: Tiny nerf: Eden has slightly less base Physical Power.
analysis.playing: Barely noticeable — a hair less early damage; play as normal.
analysis.facing: Effectively unchanged; very marginally weaker early.

analysis.summary: Feng Mao was nerfed: less penetration from his passive and lower current-health damage on his dash.
analysis.playing: You shred tanks a bit less — itemize penetration to make up for the lost armor pen.
analysis.facing: He cuts through armor less effectively now — stacking armor is a touch more reliable against him.

analysis.summary: Kallari was nerfed: less health scaling, lower dagger damage, and a longer stealth-ult cooldown.
analysis.playing: You're squishier and your engage/escape ult is up less — pick assassinations more carefully.
analysis.facing: She has more ult downtime now — group up when her stealth is on cooldown and ward to spot her.

analysis.summary: Narrow nerf: Revenant's bonus damage to monsters is capped lower, slowing his jungle and objective clear.
analysis.playing: Your camp and objective damage is a bit lower — clear is slightly slower, but your hero damage is unchanged.
analysis.facing: He takes objectives slightly slower now — contest neutrals a touch more confidently.

analysis.summary: Serath was nerfed: shorter stun, weaker empowered-mode damage, and lower Heresy burn.
analysis.playing: Your lockdown and burst are down — land Crushing Descent cleanly and don't over-rely on Fury damage.
analysis.facing: Her crowd control and burst are weaker — you have more room to fight back after her engage.

changes: Piercing Shot damage 85-245->80-220, Inner Fire scaling 70%->75%

analysis.summary: Sparrow was nerfed: less health and power, weaker passive max-health damage, and lower Piercing Shot damage.
analysis.playing: You're squishier and your poke/anti-tank damage is down — play safer and farm to your item spikes.
analysis.facing: Her early poke is weaker and she's more fragile — pressure her before she scales.

analysis.summary: The Fey was nerfed where it matters most: Verdant Embrace damage was cut significantly (a small Bramble buff doesn't offset it).
analysis.playing: Your main damage/zoning ability hits much less — play for utility and picks over raw poke.
analysis.facing: Her zoning damage is noticeably weaker — you can contest her zones more aggressively.

changes: Rising Storm max stacks 5->6 but speed 5-6.5%->3/4/5/6%
Silksong scaling 80%->75% Bonus Physical, Windburn max-health 3-6%->2-5%
Added Magical Power scaling to Lash Kick (80%) and Backlash (60%)

analysis.summary: Yin was nerfed on health and physical scaling (with Magical Power added to two abilities) — weaker for the standard build, with a possible AP flavor.
analysis.playing: Standard Yin is squishier and hits a bit less; the new Magical scaling on Lash Kick and Backlash is worth trying.
analysis.facing: He's more fragile and his physical damage dipped — pressure him and don't fear the old burst as much.
